title: Comparison of row values with COLLATE NOCASE yields incorrect result
poc: SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE (t0.c0, 0) < ('B' COLLATE NOCASE, 0);
description:
mrigger added on 2019-10-23 16:46:41:
Consider the following test case:
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 UNIQUE);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES('a');
SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE (t0.c0, 0) < ('B' COLLATE NOCASE, 0); -- unexpected: row is not fetched
Unexpectedly, the row is not fetched, even though the expression seems to evaluate to TRUE:
SELECT (t0.c0, 0) < ('B' COLLATE NOCASE, 0) FROM t0; -- 1
key_bug_features: Comparison using COLLATE NOCASE yields incorrect row fetching
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(c1 TEXT, c2 INTEGER, c3 REAL, c4 BLOB);
INSERT INTO t1(c1, c2, c3, c4) VALUES('a', 1, 1.1, X'0001');
INSERT INTO t1(c1, c2, c3, c4) VALUES('B', 2, 2.2, X'0002');
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE (t1.c1 COLLATE NOCASE, t1.c2) < ('b', 1);
SELECT (t1.c1 COLLATE NOCASE, t1.c2) < ('C', 2) FROM t1;
